name: Bruno Mars
description:
'''Peter Gene Hernandez''' (born October 8, 1985), known professionally as '''Bruno Mars''', is an American singer-songwriter, record producer and dancer. Regarded as a [[pop icon]], he is known for his three-octave [[tenor]] vocal range, live performances, [[Retro style|retro]] showmanship, and musical versatility. He is accompanied by his band, [[Bruno Mars#Band members|the Hooligans]]. Raised in [[Honolulu]], Mars gained recognition in Hawaii as a child for his [[Elvis impersonator|impersonation]] of [[Elvis Presley]], before moving to [[Los Angeles]] in 2003 to pursue a musical career.

Mars established his name in the music industry as a songwriter and co-founder of the production team [[the Smeezingtons]]. He rose to fame as a recording artist after featuring on the US number-one single "[[Nothin' on You]]" (2009) by [[B.o.B]]. Mars's first three studio albums—''[[Doo-Wops & Hooligans]]'' (2010), ''[[Unorthodox Jukebox]]'' (2012), and ''[[24K Magic (album)|24K Magic]]'' (2016)—found critical and commercial success, with the lattermost winning the [[Grammy Award for Album of the Year]]. The albums spawned multiple international hit singles, including "[[Just the Way You Are (Bruno Mars song)|Just the Way You Are]]", "[[Grenade (song)|Grenade]]", "[[The Lazy Song]]", "[[Locked Out of Heaven]]", "[[When I Was Your Man]]", "[[Treasure (song)|Treasure]]", "[[24K Magic (song)|24K Magic]]", "[[That's What I Like (Bruno Mars song)|That's What I Like]]", and "[[Finesse (Bruno Mars song)|Finesse]]". He also featured on [[Mark Ronson]]'s 2014 single "[[Uptown Funk]]", which became [[Billboard (magazine)|''Billboard'']]'s [[Billboard Decade-End|best-performing song]] of the [[List of Billboard Hot 100 number-one singles of the 2010s|2010s]].

In 2021, Mars released ''[[An Evening with Silk Sonic]]'' alongside [[Anderson .Paak]], as the [[Supergroup (music)|musical superduo]] [[Silk Sonic]]. The album contained the US number-one single "[[Leave the Door Open]]". Following two record-breaking number-one duets in 2024, "[[Die with a Smile]]" with [[Lady Gaga]] and "[[APT. (song)|APT.]]" with [[Rosé (singer)|Rosé]], Mars released his first solo album in ten years, ''[[The Romantic (album)|The Romantic]]'' (2026). It was led by his first US number-one debut, "[[I Just Might]]".

Mars has sold over 150 million records worldwide, making him one of the [[List of best-selling music artists|best-selling music artists of all time]], and he amassed [[List of Billboard Hot 100 chart achievements and milestones#Most number-one songs|ten number-one singles]] on the [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]]. He is the longest-reining act in the [[Billboard Global 200|''Billboard'' Global 200]] chart history (31 weeks) and the only artist to surpass [[List of most-streamed artists on Spotify|150 million monthly listeners]] on [[Spotify]]. His [[24K Magic World Tour]] (2017–2018) ranks among the [[List of highest-grossing concert tours|highest-grossing tours in history]]. Mars's [[List of awards and nominations received by Bruno Mars|accolades]] include 16 [[Grammy Award]]s, 14 [[American Music Awards]], 5 [[Brit Awards]], and 14 [[Soul Train Awards]]. ''[[Time (magazine)|Time]]'' named him among the [[Time 100|100 most influential people in the world]] in 2011. He was the first artist with six [[RIAA certification|RIAA Diamond-certified]] songs.
